hero.heading: Ideas worth reading.
hero.body: Competition recaps, tutorials, parent guides, and AI literacy explainers — written by coaches who've actually done the thing.

posts.title: Your first FLL season: what to expect
posts.excerpt: A parent's guide to the FIRST LEGO League season — from qualifier registration to competition day. What to prepare, and why your kid will come home exhausted and elated.
posts.author: Priya Nair
posts.imageAlt: Students competing in FIRST LEGO League

posts.title: HTML in 20 minutes: build your first web page
posts.excerpt: A step-by-step tutorial from our HTML Basics track. Open a text editor, follow along, and end with a real page live on the internet.
posts.author: Sam Torres
posts.imageAlt: Student coding HTML on a laptop

posts.title: How AI tools are changing how kids learn
posts.excerpt: AI is already in your child's classroom. Here's how to help them engage with it critically, ethically, and creatively — not just as a shortcut.
posts.author: Jordan Park

posts.title: LEGO Spike Prime vs Mindstorms: which kit is right for your child?
posts.excerpt: Both are excellent platforms — but they serve different learners. We break down the differences so you can pick the right starting point.
posts.author: Alex Chen

posts.title: What actually happens at an FLL scrimmage day?
posts.excerpt: First scrimmage coming up? Here's the full picture — match format, judging sessions, what the kids wear, and what parents should bring.
posts.author: Priya Nair
posts.imageAlt: Students at an FLL scrimmage day

posts.title: Teaching your child to think like an engineer
posts.excerpt: Engineering isn't a subject — it's a mindset. Here's how to build that mindset at home, starting from age 6, no expensive kit required.
posts.author: Alex Chen
posts.imageAlt: Child building with blocks at home
